description:
You are helping an incident-response analyst who suspects a brute-force SSH attack.  
    A sample log has already been copied to  

        /home/user/logs/auth.log  

    Your job is to produce, with a single shell-command pipeline, a brief summary that shows how many “Failed password” events came from each source IP address.

    The command you write must:

    1. Read only /home/user/logs/auth.log.  
    2. Keep only the lines that contain the exact, case-sensitive phrase  
           Failed password  
       (other log lines must be ignored).  
    3. Extract the IPv4 address that follows the word “from” on every remaining line.  
    4. Count how many times each distinct IP address appears.  
    5. Sort the result in descending numeric order by the count (largest count first).  
    6. Prepend a single header line reading exactly:  
           IP_ADDRESS␠␠ATTEMPT_COUNT  
       (that is, two ASCII space characters between the column titles).  
    7. Place (or overwrite) the result in the file  

           /home/user/ir_reports/failed_ssh_by_ip.txt  

       Create the directory /home/user/ir_reports/ if it does not yet exist.  
    8. Make sure the output file is readable by any regular user (mode 0644 or looser is fine).

    Required file format that the automatic checker will look for:

        • Line 1:     IP_ADDRESS␠␠ATTEMPT_COUNT
        • Line 2-N:   <IP>␠␠<COUNT>
                      – exactly two spaces between the two fields
                      – no leading/trailing whitespace
                      – lines sorted by COUNT in descending order
        • No blank line at the end of the file.

    You must accomplish everything above with a single compound command (a pipeline or command-group written on one logical shell line).  Do not write any explanations to the terminal; only run the command that produces the required file.
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied by the grader:

    1. Directory /home/user/logs/ exists (0755).
    2. File /home/user/logs/auth.log (0644) contains **exactly** the following 10 lines:

Jan 10 12:00:01 host sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.100 port 51234 ssh2
Jan 10 12:01:05 host sshd[12346]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 49822 ssh2
Jan 10 12:02:30 host sshd[12347]: Accepted password for john from 192.168.1.101 port 51235 ssh2
Jan 10 12:04:44 host sshd[12348]: Failed password for alice from 172.16.0.3 port 60122 ssh2
Jan 10 12:05:50 host sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.55 port 33918 ssh2
Jan 10 12:06:23 host sshd[12350]: Accepted password for jane from 192.168.1.102 port 51236 ssh2
Jan 10 12:07:10 host sshd[12351]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.100 port 51300 ssh2
Jan 10 12:07:35 host sshd[12352]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 192.168.1.100 port 51310 ssh2
Jan 10 12:08:01 host sshd[12353]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 49823 ssh2
Jan 10 12:09:15 host sshd[12354]: Failed password for admin from 192.168.1.100 port 51320 ssh2

    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

    • Directory /home/user/ir_reports/ exists (mode ≥ 0755).
    • File /home/user/ir_reports/failed_ssh_by_ip.txt exists (0644) and its exact content is:

IP_ADDRESS  ATTEMPT_COUNT
192.168.1.100  4
10.0.0.5  2
172.16.0.3  1
203.0.113.55  1

    (Each line ends with a single newline character “\n”; no additional blank lines follow.)
name: task_000000_e36bb1fc
